In contrast, individuals holding **meaningful employment elsewhere in Minnesota** are associated with slightly higher wage contexts than those in meaningful regional employment. Differences between postsecondary attendance and not-meaningful employment narrow, reinforcing that wage context plays a limited but consistent role in differentiating workforce outcomes.

**10 Years After Graduation:**\
Ten years after graduation, distinctions by wage context remain statistically significant but modest. Individuals in **meaningful regional employment** are associated with higher average county wages than those in **not-meaningful employment** or with **no Minnesota employment record**, while differences between meaningful regional employment and **meaningful employment elsewhere in Minnesota** are no longer statistically meaningful. These patterns suggest that local wage context during high school continues to differentiate stable employment outcomes from less stable or disconnected pathways, though the overall magnitude of wage differences remains small.

**Conclusion**

Average county wage levels during high school are **consistently associated with modest differences in post-graduation workforce pathways**. Across all three timeframes, individuals engaged in **meaningful regional employment** are associated with slightly higher local wage contexts than those in not-meaningful employment or without Minnesota employment records. However, these differences are **small in magnitude** and should be interpreted as reflecting **incremental contextual variation**, rather than strong separation across outcomes. Together with unemployment patterns, these findings suggest that local labor market conditions shape **where and how** graduates attach to the workforce, without exerting large or deterministic effects on individual outcomes.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs characteristics avg.wages.pct.state one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, avg.wages.pct.state) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .25),
         q3 = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .75),
         iqr = IQR(avg.wages.pct.state),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(avg.wages.pct.state > low.outlier,
         avg.wages.pct.state < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .25),
            Median = median(avg.wages.pct.state),
            Q3 = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .75),
            Max = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, 1),
            Mean = mean(avg.wages.pct.state)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.1, avg.wages.pct.state, fill = grad.year.1, group = grad.year.1)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.1, y = 1.25, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of wages as percent of state wages by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 1.5))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(avg.wages.pct.state ~ grad.year.1, data = data)

summary(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs characteristics avg.wages.pct.state five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.5, avg.wages.pct.state) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .25),
         q3 = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .75),
         iqr = IQR(avg.wages.pct.state),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(avg.wages.pct.state > low.outlier,
         avg.wages.pct.state < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .25),
            Median = median(avg.wages.pct.state),
            Q3 = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .75),
            Max = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, 1),
            Mean = mean(avg.wages.pct.state)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.5, avg.wages.pct.state, fill = grad.year.5, group = grad.year.5)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.5, y = 1.25, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of wages as percent of state wages by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 1.5))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(avg.wages.pct.state ~ grad.year.5, data = data)

summary(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs characteristics avg.wages.pct.state ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.10, avg.wages.pct.state) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .25),
         q3 = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .75),
         iqr = IQR(avg.wages.pct.state),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(avg.wages.pct.state > low.outlier,
         avg.wages.pct.state < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .25),
            Median = median(avg.wages.pct.state),
            Q3 = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, .75),
            Max = quantile(avg.wages.pct.state, 1),
            Mean = mean(avg.wages.pct.state)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.10, avg.wages.pct.state, fill = grad.year.10, group = grad.year.10)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.10, y = 1.25, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of wages as percent of state wages by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 1.5))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(avg.wages.pct.state ~ grad.year.10, data = data)

summary(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

```
:::

```{r echo=FALSE}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, grad.year.5, grad.year.10, avg.wages.pct.state) %>%
  write_csv(., "Data/ChatGPT/Employment ct/High school characteristics/avg-wages-pct-state.csv")

```

<br>

# High School Accomplishments

We are going to analyze the following variables;

-   `pseo.participant`
-   `took.ACT`
-   `ACTCompositeScore`
-   `ap.exam`
-   `sat.taken`
-   MCA scores
    -   `MCA.M`
    -   `MCA.R`
    -   `MCA.S`
-   `cte.achievement`
-   `total.cte.courses.taken`

<br>

## Summary of all high school accomplishment variables

<br>

## `pseo.participant`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between participation in Post-Secondary Enrollment Options (PSEO) and post-graduation workforce outcomes at all three timeframes:

-   **1 year after graduation:** χ²(4) = 4332.02, Cramer’s V = 0.299, *p* \< .001, *N* = 48,396\
-   **5 years after graduation:** χ²(4) = 531.91, Cramer’s V = 0.128, *p* \< .001, *N* = 32,270\
-   **10 years after graduation:** χ²(4) = 71.33, Cramer’s V = 0.076, *p* \< .001, *N* = 12,377

The relationship is **large one year after graduation**, weakens substantially by five years, and is **modest by ten years**, indicating that the association between PSEO participation and workforce outcomes is strongest early and attenuates over time.

**Interpretation Over Time**

**1 Year After Graduation:**\
One year after graduation, PSEO participation is associated with **very large differences in early postsecondary and workforce pathways**. PSEO participants are far more likely to be **attending postsecondary education** (69.5% vs. 38.8%) and substantially less likely to be engaged in **meaningful regional employment** (9.2% vs. 16.1%). They are also markedly less likely to be in **not-meaningful employment** or to have **no Minnesota employment record**. These patterns indicate that PSEO participation is closely tied to delayed workforce entry, with early pathways strongly oriented toward continued education rather than immediate regional employment.

**5 Years After Graduation:**\
By five years after graduation, differences narrow considerably, though they remain statistically significant. Individuals who did not participate in PSEO are somewhat more likely to be engaged in **meaningful regional employment** (25.0% vs. 21.1%), while PSEO participants are more likely to hold **meaningful employment elsewhere in Minnesota** (13.9% vs. 10.1%) or to still be connected to postsecondary education. PSEO participants also show lower rates of **no Minnesota employment record**. At this stage, PSEO participation appears less tied to whether individuals are employed and more related to **where employment occurs and its alignment with educational pathways**.

**10 Years After Graduation:**\
Ten years after graduation, differences by PSEO participation are **small but still evident**. Rates of **meaningful regional employment** are similar between groups (24.5% for non-participants vs. 23.4% for participants), indicating convergence in regional workforce attachment. However, PSEO participants remain more likely to hold **meaningful employment elsewhere in Minnesota** (18.4% vs. 12.6%), while non-participants are more likely to have **no Minnesota employment record** (45.8% vs. 42.8%). These patterns suggest that long-term differences associated with PSEO participation are primarily geographic rather than reflecting overall workforce attachment or employment quality within the region.

**Conclusion**

PSEO participation is **strongly associated with early post-graduation pathways**, particularly the likelihood of continued postsecondary enrollment and delayed entry into the regional workforce. Over time, this association weakens substantially, and by ten years after graduation, differences in **meaningful regional employment** largely converge. The remaining distinctions are concentrated in **employment location**, with PSEO participants more likely to work elsewhere in Minnesota and non-participants more likely to lack a Minnesota employment record. Overall, PSEO participation is closely linked to **early pathway divergence**, but it does not appear to create large or persistent differences in long-term access to meaningful regional employment.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments pseo.participant one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, pseo.participant) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023",
         pseo.participant != is.na(pseo.participant)) %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.1, var.col = data$pseo.participant, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE)
```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments pseo.participant five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.5, pseo.participant) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023",
         pseo.participant != is.na(pseo.participant)) %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.5, var.col = data$pseo.participant, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE)
```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments pseo.participant ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.10, pseo.participant) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023",
         pseo.participant != is.na(pseo.participant)) %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.10, var.col = data$pseo.participant, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE)
```
:::

```{r echo=FALSE}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, grad.year.5, grad.year.10, pseo.participant) %>%
  write_csv(., "Data/ChatGPT/Employment ct/High school accomplishments/pseo-participant.csv")

```

<br>

## `took.ACT`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between taking the ACT in high school and post-graduation workforce outcomes at all three timeframes:

-   **1 year after graduation:** χ²(4) = 8,929.54, Cramer’s V = 0.381, *p* \< .001, *N* = 61,485\
-   **5 years after graduation:** χ²(4) = 1,909.75, Cramer’s V = 0.205, *p* \< .001, *N* = 45,359\
-   **10 years after graduation:** χ²(4) = 462.42, Cramer’s V = 0.135, *p* \< .001, *N* = 25,464

The relationship is **very large one year after graduation**, weakens by five years, and is **moderate by ten years**, indicating substantial early pathway divergence that narrows over time but does not fully disappear.

**Interpretation Over Time**

**1 Year After Graduation:**\
One year after graduation, taking the ACT is associated with **very large differences in early post-graduation pathways**, including access to **meaningful regional employment**. Individuals who did **not** take the ACT are far more likely to be engaged in **meaningful regional employment** (18.8% vs. 10.4%) and in **not-meaningful employment** or to have **no Minnesota employment record**. In contrast, individuals who took the ACT are overwhelmingly more likely to be **attending postsecondary education** (64.3% vs. 24.4%). These patterns indicate that ACT-taking is closely aligned with delayed workforce entry and postsecondary-oriented pathways, while non–ACT-takers are more likely to enter the regional workforce immediately, often in lower-quality or less stable employment.

**5 Years After Graduation:**\
By five years after graduation, differences narrow but remain substantial. Individuals who did not take the ACT continue to show higher rates of **meaningful regional employment** (27.4% vs. 21.4%), while ACT-takers are more likely to hold **meaningful employment elsewhere in Minnesota** (12.4% vs. 8.3%) or to remain connected to postsecondary education. Non–ACT-takers also exhibit higher rates of **no Minnesota employment record** and **not-meaningful employment**, suggesting that early workforce entry is associated with greater long-term instability even as regional attachment remains stronger.

**10 Years After Graduation:**\
Ten years after graduation, the association weakens further but remains evident. Individuals who did not take the ACT are still more likely to be engaged in **meaningful regional employment** (28.0% vs. 21.9%), while ACT-takers are substantially more likely to hold **meaningful employment elsewhere in Minnesota** (18.0% vs. 9.7%). Rates of **no Minnesota employment record** are high for both groups but slightly higher among ACT-takers. At this stage, differences associated with ACT-taking are primarily **geographic**, reflecting where individuals work rather than whether they are attached to the workforce.

**Conclusion**

Taking the ACT is **one of the most strongly differentiating high school accomplishments with respect to early post-graduation pathways**, particularly in the first year after graduation. Individuals who do not take the ACT are much more likely to enter the regional workforce immediately, including into **meaningful regional employment**, but they are also more exposed to **not-meaningful work and long-term instability**. Over time, differences in workforce participation narrow, and by ten years after graduation, distinctions are concentrated primarily in **employment location**, with ACT-takers more likely to work elsewhere in Minnesota. Overall, ACT-taking reflects early pathway sorting that shapes **when and where** individuals engage with the workforce, rather than producing large long-term differences in overall workforce attachment.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments took.ACT one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, took.ACT) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.1, var.col = data$took.ACT, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE)
```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments took.ACT five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.5, took.ACT) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.5, var.col = data$took.ACT, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE)
```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments took.ACT ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.10, took.ACT) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.10, var.col = data$took.ACT, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE)
```
:::

```{r echo = FALSE}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, grad.year.5, grad.year.10, took.ACT) %>%
  write_csv(., "Data/ChatGPT/Employment ct/High school accomplishments/took-ACT.csv")

```

<br>

## `ACTCompositeScore`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between ACT Composite Score and post-graduation workforce outcomes at all three timeframes:

-   **1 year after graduation:** F(4, 41,497) = 866.8, *p* \< .001\
-   **5 years after graduation:** F(4, 30,550) = 323.1, *p* \< .001\
-   **10 years after graduation:** F(3, 15,458) = 131.5, *p* \< .001

Across all three timepoints, mean ACT Composite Scores differ substantially by workforce outcome category. Compared to most other variables examined, ACT Composite Score shows **one of the clearest and most persistent separations** across post-graduation pathways.

**Interpretation Over Time**

**1 Year After Graduation:**\
One year after graduation, ACT Composite Score is **strongly differentiated across early post-graduation pathways**, including access to **meaningful regional employment**. Individuals who are **attending postsecondary education** have ACT scores that are more than three points higher, on average, than those engaged in meaningful regional employment. Individuals in **meaningful regional employment**, **not-meaningful employment**, and those with **no Minnesota employment record** have relatively similar ACT scores, all substantially lower than those of postsecondary attendees. These patterns indicate that early entry into the regional workforce—whether meaningful or not—is associated with lower ACT performance relative to postsecondary-oriented pathways.

**5 Years After Graduation:**\
By five years after graduation, differences across outcome groups remain pronounced. Individuals holding **meaningful employment elsewhere in Minnesota** or those who were **attending postsecondary education** have ACT scores more than two points higher, on average, than those in **meaningful regional employment**. Individuals in **not-meaningful employment** and those with **no Minnesota employment record** continue to cluster below these groups, though meaningful regional employment occupies an intermediate position. At this stage, ACT Composite Score is closely associated with **both employment quality and employment location**, distinguishing regional from non-regional pathways.

**10 Years After Graduation:**\
Ten years after graduation, ACT score differences persist, though the overall separation narrows slightly. Individuals engaged in **meaningful employment elsewhere in Minnesota** continue to have ACT scores nearly two points higher than those in **meaningful regional employment**, while individuals with **no Minnesota employment record** also exhibit higher ACT scores than regional workers. Differences between meaningful regional employment and **not-meaningful employment** are smaller but still statistically significant. These patterns suggest that long-term regional workforce attachment is most common among individuals with lower ACT scores, while higher-scoring individuals are more likely to work outside the region or exit Minnesota employment records.

**Conclusion**

ACT Composite Score is **one of the most strongly differentiating high school accomplishments** with respect to post-graduation workforce pathways. Across all three timeframes, higher ACT scores are consistently associated with postsecondary-oriented pathways and with meaningful employment outside the Northwest region. In contrast, **meaningful regional employment is concentrated among individuals with comparatively lower ACT scores**, a pattern that persists even ten years after graduation. These findings underscore a central challenge for the region: retaining academically high-performing graduates in **meaningful regional employment**, while also ensuring that early workforce entrants are not systematically limited to lower-quality or less stable employment pathways.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments ACTCompositeScore one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, ACTCompositeScore) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .25),
         q3 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .75),
         iqr = IQR(ACTCompositeScore),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(ACTCompositeScore > low.outlier,
         ACTCompositeScore < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .25),
            Median = median(ACTCompositeScore),
            Q3 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .75),
            Max = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, 1),
            Mean = mean(ACTCompositeScore)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.1, ACTCompositeScore, fill = grad.year.1, group = grad.year.1)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.1, y = 40, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of ACT composite score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 45))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(ACTCompositeScore ~ grad.year.1, data = data)

summary(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments ACTCompositeScore five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.5, ACTCompositeScore) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .25),
         q3 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .75),
         iqr = IQR(ACTCompositeScore),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(ACTCompositeScore > low.outlier,
         ACTCompositeScore < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .25),
            Median = median(ACTCompositeScore),
            Q3 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .75),
            Max = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, 1),
            Mean = mean(ACTCompositeScore)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.5, ACTCompositeScore, fill = grad.year.5, group = grad.year.5)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.5, y = 40, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of ACT composite score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 45))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(ACTCompositeScore ~ grad.year.5, data = data)

summary(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments ACTCompositeScore ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.10, ACTCompositeScore) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .25),
         q3 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .75),
         iqr = IQR(ACTCompositeScore),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(ACTCompositeScore > low.outlier,
         ACTCompositeScore < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .25),
            Median = median(ACTCompositeScore),
            Q3 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .75),
            Max = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, 1),
            Mean = mean(ACTCompositeScore)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.10, ACTCompositeScore, fill = grad.year.10, group = grad.year.10)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.10, y = 40, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of ACT composite score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 40))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(ACTCompositeScore ~ grad.year.10, data = data)

summary(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

```
:::

```{r echo=FALSE}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, grad.year.5, grad.year.10, ACTCompositeScore) %>%
  write_csv(., "Data/ChatGPT/Employment ct/High school accomplishments/ACTCompositeScore.csv")

```

<br>

## `ap.exam`

### **Key Findings**

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments ap.exam one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, ap.exam) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.1, var.col = data$ap.exam, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE)
```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments ap.exam five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.5, ap.exam) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.5, var.col = data$ap.exam, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE)
```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments ap.exam ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.10, ap.exam) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.10, var.col = data$ap.exam, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE)
```
:::

```{r echo=FALSE}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, grad.year.5, grad.year.10, ap.exam) %>%
  write_csv(., "Data/ChatGPT/Employment ct/High school accomplishments/ap-exam.csv")

```

<br>

## `MCA.R`

### **Key Findings**

MCA Reading scores are **strongly associated** with post-graduation outcomes:

-   **1 year after graduation**: F = **3405.65**, p \< .001
-   **5 years after graduation**: F = **395.20**, p \< .001
-   **10 years after graduation**: F = **812.59**, p \< .001

The association is strongest in the **first year**, weakens by 5 years, but remains highly significant even after 10 years.

### **Interpretation Over Time**

**1 Year After Graduation**

-   Postsecondary students: **3.60** average MCA-R
-   Meaningful regional employment: **3.26**
-   Meaningful MN employment: **3.29**
-   Not meaningful, not attending PS: **3.00**
-   Disconnected: **2.90**

Higher reading scores are most strongly associated with postsecondary enrollment, while **lower scores are tied to regional employment and disconnection**.

**5 Years After Graduation**

-   Postsecondary: **3.61**
-   Meaningful MN employment: **3.52**
-   Meaningful regional employment: **3.35**
-   Not meaningful: **3.37**
-   Disconnected: **3.49**

Students with higher scores remain more engaged in postsecondary or in meaningful employment outside the region. Those with **lower scores are more likely to be in regional employment or in not-meaningful work**.

**10 Years After Graduation**

-   Meaningful MN employment: **3.4**
-   Meaningful regional employment: **3.04**
-   Not meaningful: **3.06**
-   Disconnected: **3.45**

By 10 years, the divide is clear: **graduates with higher reading scores are more likely to hold meaningful employment outside the region**, while those with lower scores are more concentrated in regional employment or less meaningful roles.

### **Conclusion**

MCA Reading performance is a **persistent predictor of post-graduation outcomes**. Higher scores consistently lead to **postsecondary enrollment and meaningful employment outside the region**, while lower scores are linked to **regional workforce participation and elevated risks of disconnection or not-meaningful work**. This highlights reading proficiency as an important indicator of both **educational attainment and long-term geographic mobility**.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.R one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, MCA.R) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.R, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.R, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.R),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.R > low.outlier,
         MCA.R < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.R, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.R, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.R),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.R, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.R, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.R)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.1, MCA.R, fill = grad.year.1, group = grad.year.1)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.1, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Reading score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(MCA.R ~ grad.year.1, data = data)

summary(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.R five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.5, MCA.R) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.R, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.R, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.R),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.R > low.outlier,
         MCA.R < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.R, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.R, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.R),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.R, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.R, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.R)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.5, MCA.R, fill = grad.year.5, group = grad.year.5)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.5, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Reading score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(MCA.R ~ grad.year.5, data = data)

summary(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

```

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.R ten year}
         MCA.R < high.outlier) %>%

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.R, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.R, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.R),

            Max = quantile(MCA.R, 1),

  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
```

:::

```{r echo=FALSE}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, grad.year.5, grad.year.10, MCA.R) %>%
  write_csv(., "Data/ChatGPT/Employment ct/High school accomplishments/MCA-R.csv")

```

<br>

## `MCA.S`

### **Key Findings**

MCA Science scores are **strongly associated** with post-graduation outcomes:

-   **1 year after graduation**: F = **2162.51**, p \< .001
-   **5 years after graduation**: F = **440.14**, p \< .001
-   **10 years after graduation**: F = **181.42**, p \< .001

The association is strongest in the **first year**, but remains statistically significant across all timeframes.

### **Interpretation Over Time**

**1 Year After Graduation**

-   Postsecondary students: **2.89** average MCA-S
-   Meaningful regional employment: **2.40**
-   Meaningful MN employment: **2.39**
-   Not meaningful, not attending PS: **2.33**
-   Disconnected: **2.35**

Higher science scores are closely tied to postsecondary enrollment, while **lower scores are linked to early regional employment, not-meaningful work, or disconnection**.

**5 Years After Graduation**

-   Postsecondary: **2.87**
-   Meaningful MN employment: **2.73**
-   Meaningful regional employment: **2.51**
-   Not meaningful: **2.47**
-   Disconnected: **2.69**

By this stage, higher scores remain associated with continued postsecondary enrollment and employment outside the region. Lower scorers are more likely to be in **regional employment or not-meaningful work**.

**10 Years After Graduation**

-   Meaningful MN employment: **2.66**
-   Meaningful regional employment: **2.42**
-   Not meaningful: **2.41**
-   Disconnected: **2.65**

A decade later, the divide persists: **graduates with higher science scores are more likely to hold meaningful employment outside the region**, while those with lower scores are more likely to stay in the regional workforce or in not-meaningful roles.

### **Conclusion**

MCA Science scores are a **strong and persistent predictor** of post-graduation outcomes. Higher scores consistently lead to **postsecondary enrollment and meaningful employment beyond the region**, while lower scores are associated with **regional workforce participation and elevated risks of not-meaningful work or disconnection**. This underscores science proficiency as a marker of both **educational attainment and long-term labor market mobility**.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.S five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.5, MCA.S) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.S, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.S, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.S),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.S > low.outlier,
         MCA.S < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.S, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.S, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.S),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.S, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.S, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.S)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.5, MCA.S, fill = grad.year.5, group = grad.year.5)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.5, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Science score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(MCA.S ~ grad.year.5, data = data)

summary(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.S ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.10, MCA.S) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.S, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.S, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.S),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.S > low.outlier,
         MCA.S < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.S, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.S, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.S),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.S, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.S, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.S)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.10, MCA.S, fill = grad.year.10, group = grad.year.10)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.10, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Science score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+

  theme_bar+

        text = element_text(size = 18),
```

:::

```{r echo=FALSE}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, grad.year.5, grad.year.10, MCA.S) %>%
  write_csv(., "Data/ChatGPT/Employment ct/High school accomplishments/MCA-S.csv")

```

<br>

## `MCA.M`

### **Key Findings**

MCA Math scores are **very strongly associated** with post-graduation outcomes:

-   **1 year after graduation**: F = **4955.39**, p \< .001
-   **5 years after graduation**: F = **1487.88**, p \< .001
-   **10 years after graduation**: F = **316.96**, p \< .001

The association is strongest in the **first year**, and while it weakens over time, it remains highly significant even after 10 years.

### **Interpretation Over Time**

**1 Year After Graduation**

-   Postsecondary students: **3.40** average MCA-M
-   Meaningful MN employment: **2.75**
-   Meaningful regional employment: **2.74**
-   Not meaningful, not attending PS: **2.60**
-   Disconnected (no MN record): **2.52**

Students with higher math scores overwhelmingly attend postsecondary, while those with lower scores are more likely to enter the regional workforce or face disconnection. This shows that **math proficiency is a strong gateway into higher education, while lower scores push students toward the local labor market**.

**5 Years After Graduation**

-   Postsecondary students: **3.35**
-   Meaningful MN employment: **3.29**
-   Meaningful regional employment: **2.76**
-   Not meaningful: **2.68**
-   Disconnected: **2.90**

The divide continues: higher scorers remain more likely in postsecondary or employed outside the region, while lower scorers are tied to regional employment or not-meaningful work. Regional employment remains concentrated among students with lower math scores.

**10 Years After Graduation**

-   Meaningful MN employment: **2.78**
-   Disconnected: **2.74**
-   Meaningful regional employment: **2.44**
-   Not meaningful: **2.43**

Even a decade later, **regional employment is associated with the lowest math scores**, while graduates with stronger scores are more likely to hold meaningful employment outside the region. This highlights the long-term sorting effect of math proficiency on geographic and labor market outcomes.

### **Conclusion**

MCA Math scores are a **powerful and lasting predictor** of post-graduation pathways. Higher scores consistently lead to **postsecondary enrollment and meaningful employment beyond Southern Minnesota**, while lower scores anchor students more strongly in the **regional workforce** or expose them to risks of disconnection. This underscores math proficiency as a critical determinant of both **educational mobility and regional labor market retention**.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.M one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.northwest %>%
  select(grad.year.1, MCA.M) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.M, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.M, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.M),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.M > low.outlier,
         MCA.M < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.M, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.M, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.M),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.M, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.M, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.M)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.1, MCA.M, fill = grad.year.1, group = grad.year.1)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.1, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Math score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(MCA.M ~ grad.year.1, data = data)

summary(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.M ten year}
  select(grad.year.10, MCA.M) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%

            Median = median(MCA.M),

            Max = quantile(MCA.M, 1),

  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.10, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Math score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
```
